summary: Clear soundboard capture of a late‑'77 show with an outrageous Lazy Lightning > Supplication > Deal combo, stellar Peggy‑O and a top‑tier The Other One.
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source that faithfully renders the band’s onstage banter and Keith's sweet harpsichord tones. Musically the night shines on the second set – Lazy Lightning into Supplication and Deal explode, Peggy‑O dazzles, and The Other One ranks among the best of the era, despite a few slower moments in the first set.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ~4.1 with consistent praise for both performance and recording quality; many detailed positive comments outweigh a few minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix that captures stage details (Jerry’s banter, Keith’s harpsichord) with minimal noise; reviewers note it as a "great sound" and superior to low‑gen cassette sources.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as slow to get going, picking up toward the end with Lazy Lightning/Supplication and Deal. The second set opens strong (Samson) and delivers a superb flow: Peggy‑O, a jam around Playin', Wheel > Wharf Rat, and an epic Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo work is praised as "wailing"; his sassy pre‑Lazy Lightning comment and banter are highlighted, though one reviewer felt he sounded a bit out of it on Candyman.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned for suggesting the Wheel > Wharf Rat jam; otherwise no specific comments on bass playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s “tinkling sweetly on the pseudo‑harpsichord” and overall keyboard work receive strong praise.
